name: Dark Chocolate Truffles
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the chopped semisweet and unsweetened chocolate in a 4-quart bowl.
2. Heat heavy cream in a 1 1/2 quart saucepan over medium heat until it boils.
3. Pour the boiling cream over the chocolate, allow to stand for 5 minutes.
4. Stir until smooth (ganache formed). Refrigerate for 1 hour until firm but not hard.
5. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
6. Portion out ganache into tablespoons to make 24 truffles (about 3/4 ounce each).
7. Space the truffles evenly on the lined baking sheet and refrigerate for 15 minutes.
8. Roll each ganache portion gently in circular motions using your palms until smooth.
9. Coat half of the truffles with cocoa and the other half with confectioners' sugar.
10. Store the truffles in a tightly sealed plastic container in the refrigerator.
11. Remove from the refrigerator about 1 hour before serving.
